title: Train a groupwise ranking model for AUC
summary: ROC AUC evaluates ordering, not probability calibration.
rationale: ROC AUC evaluates ordering, not probability calibration. A pairwise/listwise ranker grouped by Race_Year or Race_Year-Driver can focus on ranking true pit-next laps above many adjacent non-pit laps, which ordinary binary logloss may underemphasize.
implementation_hint: Add a CatBoost PairLogitPairwise or LightGBM ranker branch using the same engineered features; group rows by race-year or driver-race stint, output raw decision scores, and blend with the existing classifier by OOF AUC.
expected_effect: Small AUC lift from better within-race ordering, especially around dense pit windows where probabilities are close.
risk: Pairwise objectives can overfit groups or produce poorly scaled scores; compare with rank-normalized OOF predictions, not raw probabilities.
